items.title: Word of the Day
items.text:
Learn a new word every day, right inside Obsidian.

Word of the Day fetches Merriam-Webster's Word of the Day and saves it as a clean, structured note in your vault. Optionally generate spaced-repetition flashcards with Osmosis so you actually remember what you learn.

items.title: What It Does
items.text:
1. Fetches the Word of the Day from Merriam-Webster's RSS feed
  2. Creates a note in your vault with pronunciation, definition, examples, and etymology
  3. Optionally embeds an Osmosis flashcard so you can study the word with spaced repetition

Each word gets its own file -- no date prefixes, no clutter. Just fiscal.md, ephemeral.md, serendipity.md.

items.title: Getting Started
items.text:
New to Word of the Day? Start here:

  • Installation -- how to install the plugin
  • Quick Start -- fetch your first word in under a minute

items.title: Configuration
items.text:
• Settings -- all available settings and what they do
  • Callout Styles -- native callouts vs. Admonitions plugin

items.title: Osmosis Integration
items.text:
• Flashcard Types -- cloze, basic, and bidirectional cards
  • Decks -- assign words to Osmosis decks

items.title: Changelog
items.text:
All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on Keep a Changelog, and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

items.title: Added
items.text:
• Fetch Merriam-Webster's Word of the Day from RSS
  • Structured notes with pronunciation, definition, examples, and etymology
  • Osmosis flashcard integration (cloze, basic, bidirectional)
  • Deck assignment via frontmatter
  • Native Obsidian callouts or Admonitions plugin syntax
  • Folder auto-suggest for destination folder setting
  • Insert WOTD link at cursor command
  • Ribbon icon using message-square-quote

items.title: Configuration
items.text:
Customize how Word of the Day creates and formats your notes.

  • Settings -- all available settings and their defaults
  • Callout Styles -- choose between native callouts and Admonitions

items.title: Callout Styles
items.text: Word of the Day uses callout blocks for the "Examples" and "Did you know?" sections. You can choose between two formats.

items.title: Callouts (Built-in)
items.text:
Obsidian's native callout syntax. No additional plugins required.

> [!example] Examples
> The governor proposed a number of *fiscal* reforms.

> [!question] Did you know?
> Fiscal derives from the Latin word "fiscus," meaning "basket"
> or "treasury."

This is the default setting.

items.title: Admonitions (Plugin)
items.text:
Uses the code fence syntax from the Admonitions community plugin. Choose this if you already use Admonitions in your vault.

```ad-example
title: Examples
The governor proposed a number of *fiscal* reforms.
```

```ad-question
title: Did you know?
Fiscal derives from the Latin word "fiscus," meaning "basket"
or "treasury."
```

Note

The Admonitions plugin must be installed and enabled for this syntax to render correctly.

items.title: Changing the Style
items.text:
1. Open Settings > Word of the Day
  2. Under Callout Style, select your preferred format
  3. Fetch a new word (or re-fetch the current one) to see the change

Existing notes are not automatically updated when you change this setting. Re-fetch a word to regenerate its note with the new style.

items.title: Settings
items.text: Open Settings > Word of the Day to configure the plugin.

items.title: Destination Folder
items.text:
The folder where WOTD notes are created. The folder is created automatically if it doesn't exist. As you type, existing vault folders are suggested.

  • Default: WOTD

items.title: Include HTML from RSS
items.text:
When enabled, the full Merriam-Webster description is parsed and included in the note body -- definition, usage examples, "Did you know?" section, and a link to the dictionary entry.

When disabled, only the frontmatter and heading are generated.

  • Default: On

items.title: Callout Style
items.text:
Choose how the "Examples" and "Did you know?" sections are formatted. See Callout Styles for details.

  • Default: Callouts (built-in)

items.title: Osmosis Flashcards
items.text: Optional integration with Osmosis for spaced-repetition study. See Flashcard Types and Decks.

items.title: Include Osmosis Flashcard
items.text:
Toggle this on to embed a flashcard at the bottom of each WOTD note.

  • Default: Off

items.title: Flashcard Type
items.text:
Choose the type of flashcard to generate. Only visible when flashcards are enabled.

Type Description Cloze Fill-in-the-blank -- the word is highlighted with ==markers== Basic Front/back -- "What does word mean?" on the front, definition on the back Bidirectional Study in both directions -- word on one side, definition on the other
  • Default: Bidirectional

items.title: Deck
items.text:
Assign generated flashcards to an Osmosis deck by adding osmosis-deck to the note's frontmatter. Leave blank for no deck assignment.

  • Default: (none)
  • Example: Vocab/Word of the Day

items.title: Test Fetch
items.text: A button that runs the full fetch-and-create workflow immediately. Useful for verifying your settings are correct.

items.title: Getting Started
items.text:
Get Word of the Day up and running in your Obsidian vault.

  • Installation -- install from Community Plugins or manually
  • Quick Start -- fetch your first word in under a minute

items.title: From Community Plugins (Coming Soon)
items.text:
1. Open Settings > Community Plugins
  2. Click Browse and search for "Word of the Day"
  3. Click Install, then Enable

items.title: Manual Installation
items.text:
1. Download the latest release from GitHub Releases
  2. Extract main.js, styles.css, and manifest.json into your vault at: 
    .obsidian/plugins/wotd/
  3. Restart Obsidian (or reload plugins)
  4. Enable Word of the Day in Settings > Community Plugins

items.title: Verify Installation
items.text:
After enabling the plugin, you should see:

  • A quote icon () in the left ribbon
  • Word of the Day in the settings sidebar
  • Three new commands in the command palette (search "WOTD")

items.title: Fetch Your First Word
items.text:
1. Click the icon in the left ribbon
  2. A notification confirms the word was saved
  3. Find the new note in your destination folder (default: WOTD/)

That's it. The note includes the word's pronunciation, part of speech, definition, usage examples, and etymology.

items.title: Open the Note Directly
items.text: Use the command palette (Ctrl+P) and run Open today's WOTD note. This fetches the word (if needed) and opens it in a new pane.

items.title: Insert a Link
items.text: While editing any note, run Insert link to today's WOTD at cursor from the command palette. A [[link]] to today's word is inserted at your cursor position.

items.title: Example Note
items.text:
Here's what a typical WOTD note looks like:

---
word: fiscal
definition: Fiscal is used to describe things relating to money...
source: https://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/fiscal
published: 2026-03-28
---

# fiscal

---

**fiscal** • \fi-skəl\ • *adjective*

Fiscal is used to describe things relating to money and especially
to the money a government, business, or organization earns, spends,
and owes.

> [!example] Examples
> The governor proposed a number of *fiscal* reforms.

> [!question] Did you know?
> Fiscal derives from the Latin word "fiscus," meaning "basket" or
> "treasury."

items.title: Next Steps
items.text:
• Settings -- customize the destination folder, callout style, and more
  • Osmosis Integration -- add spaced-repetition flashcards to your notes

items.title: Osmosis Integration
items.text:
Word of the Day integrates with Osmosis, a spaced-repetition flashcard plugin for Obsidian. When enabled, each WOTD note includes a flashcard so you can study and retain new vocabulary.

  • Flashcard Types -- cloze, basic, and bidirectional cards
  • Decks -- organize your word flashcards into Osmosis decks

Info

Osmosis must be installed and enabled for flashcards to work. See the Osmosis documentation for setup instructions.

items.title: Decks
items.text: You can assign WOTD flashcards to a specific Osmosis deck. This keeps your vocabulary cards organized alongside your other study material.

items.title: Setting a Deck
items.text:
1. Open Settings > Word of the Day
  2. Toggle Include Osmosis flashcard on
  3. Enter a deck name in the Deck field (e.g., Vocab/Word of the Day)

When a deck is set, Word of the Day adds an osmosis-deck field to the note's frontmatter:

---
word: fiscal
definition: Fiscal is used to describe things...
source: https://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/fiscal
published: 2026-03-28
osmosis-deck: Vocab/Word of the Day
---

Osmosis reads this frontmatter to assign all cards in the note to the specified deck.

items.title: Nested Decks
items.text:
Osmosis supports nested decks using / as a separator. For example:

  • Vocab -- a top-level deck
  • Vocab/Word of the Day -- a sub-deck under Vocab
  • Vocab/Word of the Day/2026 -- a further sub-deck

items.title: No Deck
items.text: Leave the Deck field blank to skip deck assignment. Cards will appear in the default deck (or be organized by whatever rules you've configured in Osmosis).

items.title: Learn More
items.text: See the Osmosis deck documentation for more on how decks work.

items.title: Flashcard Types
items.text: Word of the Day can generate three types of Osmosis flashcards. Choose your preferred type in Settings > Word of the Day > Flashcard type.

items.title: Cloze
items.text:
A fill-in-the-blank card. The word is wrapped in ==highlight markers== within the definition. Osmosis blanks out the highlighted term during study.

```osmosis
==fiscal== is used to describe things relating to money and especially
to the money a government, business, or organization earns, spends,
and owes.
```

Best for: Recognizing the word in context.

items.title: Basic (Front / Back)
items.text:
A simple question-and-answer card. The front asks "What does word mean?" and the back shows the definition.

```osmosis
What does **fiscal** mean?
***
Is used to describe things relating to money and especially to the
money a government, business, or organization earns, spends, and owes.
```

Best for: Recall-based study -- can you produce the definition from memory?

items.title: Bidirectional
items.text:
Like a basic card, but Osmosis creates two cards: one from word to definition, and one from definition to word. The definition side does not contain the word itself, so it works cleanly in both directions.

```osmosis
bidi: true

fiscal
***
Is used to describe things relating to money and especially to the
money a government, business, or organization earns, spends, and owes.
```

Best for: Full vocabulary mastery -- recognize the word and recall it from its meaning.

This is the default flashcard type.
